question.text: An inter-commodity spread between guar seed and guar gum exploits:

answer.text: The economic relationship between raw material (guar seed) and its derivative product (guar gum)
Calendar differences between the same commodity
Regional price differences in the same commodity

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Inter-commodity spreads trade related commodities — guar seed (input) and guar gum (output) have an economic processing relationship, so their prices are correlated. **The Trap:** Calendar differences are INTRA-commodity. Currency and regional differences are other arbitrage types. **Cross-Exam Context:** Input-output commodity relationships are specific to commodity markets. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Understanding the seed-to-gum conversion ratio helps estimate fair spread — if gum/seed ratio deviates from historical processing margins, spread trading opportunities arise.
